net.openhft.chronicle.core.util

Class Longs

  • java.lang.Object
    • net.openhft.chronicle.core.util.Longs

  • public final class Longs
extends Object
    A collection of functional compositions to check and assert various requirements related to long values.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static boolean assertIfEnabled(LongBiPredicate requirement, long value, long otherValue)
      Asserts that the provided value and provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement if assertions is enabled (i.e.
      static boolean assertIfEnabled(LongPredicate requirement, long value)
      Asserts that the provided value satisfies the provided requirement if assertions is enabled (i.e.
      static boolean assertIfEnabled(LongTriPredicate requirement, long value, long otherFirstValue, long otherSecondValue)
      Asserts that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherSecondValue satisfies the provided requirement if assertions is enabled (i.e.
      static LongTriPredicate between()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between another first value (inclusive) and another second value (exclusive) (i.e value ∈ [other first value , other second value) ).
      static LongTriPredicate betweenClosed()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between (closed) another first value (inclusive) and another second value (inclusive) (i.e value ∈ [other first value , other second value] ).
      static LongBiPredicate betweenZeroAnd()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another value (exclusive) (i.e value ∈ [0, other value) ).
      static LongBiPredicate betweenZeroAndClosed()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another value (inclusive) (i.e value ∈ [0, other value] ).
      static LongTriPredicate betweenZeroAndReserving()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another first value (inclusive) whilst ensuring a value of size defined by another second value can fit.
      static LongPredicate byteConvertible()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value can fit in a byte (i.e.
      static LongBiPredicate equalTo()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is equal to another value.
      static LongPredicate evenPowerOfTwo()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is an even power of two (i.e.
      static String failDescription(LongBiPredicate requirement, long value, long otherValue)
      Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value and provided otherValue did not satisfy the provided requirement.
      static String failDescription(LongPredicate requirement, long value)
      Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value did not satisfy the provided requirement.
      static String failDescription(LongTriPredicate requirement, long value, long otherFirstValue, long otherSecondValue)
      Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherFirstValue did not satisfy the provided requirement.

      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is greater or equal to another value.
      static LongBiPredicate greaterThan()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is greater than to another value.
      static LongBiPredicate lessOrEqual()
      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is less or equal to another value.

      Returns a predicate that can test if a value is less than to another value.

      Returns the provided value after checking that it and the provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

      Returns the provided value after checking that it and the provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

      Returns the provided value after checking that it satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

      long      		 require(LongPredicate requirement, long value, Function<String,X> exceptionMapper)
      Returns the provided value after checking that it satisfies the provided requirement throwing a custom exception if the check fails.

      Returns the provided value after checking that it and the provided otherValue and the provided otherSecondValue satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

      Returns the provided value after checking that it is non-negative throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Method Detail

      • requireNonNegative

        public static long requireNonNegative(long value)
        Returns the provided value after checking that it is non-negative throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(long bar) {
     this.bar = requireNonNegative(bar);
 }

        This method is functionally equivalent to: 

             require(negative().negate(), value);
        but is potentially optimized for performance.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • require

        public static long require(LongPredicate requirement,
                           long value)
        Returns the provided value after checking that it satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(long bar) {
     this.bar = require(nonNegative(), bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value
        value - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • require

        public static <X extends RuntimeException> long require(LongPredicate requirement,
                                                        long value,
                                                        Function<String,X> exceptionMapper)
        Returns the provided value after checking that it satisfies the provided requirement throwing a custom exception if the check fails.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(long bar) {
     this.bar = require(byteConvertible(), bar, ArithmeticException::new);
 }
        Type Parameters:
        X - Exception typ to throw if the check fails
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value
        value - the value to check
        exceptionMapper - to apply should the check fail
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null or if the provided exceptionMapper is null.
        RuntimeException - of the specified type of the provided exceptionMapper

      • require

        public static long require(LongBiPredicate requirement,
                           long value,
                           long otherValue)
        Returns the provided value after checking that it and the provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

        Checks that the provided value and provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(long bar) {
     this.bar = require(betweenZeroAnd(), bar, 32);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value and otherValue
        value - the value to check
        otherValue - the other value to compare against the provided value
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • require

        public static <X extends RuntimeException> long require(LongBiPredicate requirement,
                                                        long value,
                                                        long otherValue,
                                                        Function<String,X> exceptionMapper)
        Returns the provided value after checking that it and the provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

        Checks that the provided value and provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(long bar) {
     this.bar = require(betweenZeroAnd(), bar, 16, 32, ArithmeticException::new);
 }
        Type Parameters:
        X - Exception typ to throw if the check fails
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value and otherValue
        value - the value to check
        otherValue - the other value to compare against the provided value
        exceptionMapper - to apply should the check fail
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • require

        public static long require(LongTriPredicate requirement,
                           long value,
                           long otherFirstValue,
                           long otherSecondValue)
        Returns the provided value after checking that it and the provided otherValue and the provided otherSecondValue satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(long bar) {
     this.bar = require(between(), bar, 16, 32);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        otherFirstValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        otherSecondValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • require

        public static <X extends RuntimeException> long require(LongTriPredicate requirement,
                                                        long value,
                                                        long otherFirstValue,
                                                        long otherSecondValue,
                                                        Function<String,X> exceptionMapper)
        Returns the provided value after checking that it and the provided otherValue and the provided otherSecondValue satisfies the provided requirement throwing an IllegalArgumentException if the check fails.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(long bar) {
     this.bar = require(between(), bar, 16, 32, ArithmeticException::new);
 }
        Type Parameters:
        X - Exception typ to throw if the check fails
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        otherFirstValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        otherSecondValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        exceptionMapper - to apply should the check fail
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • assertIfEnabled

        public static boolean assertIfEnabled(LongPredicate requirement,
                                      long value)
        Asserts that the provided value satisfies the provided requirement if assertions is enabled (i.e. -ea) and AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS is false.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in private methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         private Foo(long bar) {
     assertIfEnabled(nonNegative, bar);
     this.bar = bar;
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value
        value - the value to check
        Returns:
        true
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null. There is no guarantee that this exception is thrown. For example, if assertions are not enabled, then the exception might not be thrown.
        AssertionError - if the check fails and assertions are enabled both via the -ea JVM command line option and by setting AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS to false.

      • assertIfEnabled

        public static boolean assertIfEnabled(LongBiPredicate requirement,
                                      long value,
                                      long otherValue)
        Asserts that the provided value and provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement if assertions is enabled (i.e. -ea) and AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS is false.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in private methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         private Foo(long bar) {
     assertIfEnabled(betweenZeroAnd(), bar, 32);
     this.bar = bar;
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value and otherValue
        value - the value to check
        otherValue - the other value to compare against the provided value
        Returns:
        true
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null. There is no guarantee that this exception is thrown. For example, if assertions are not enabled, then the exception might not be thrown.
        AssertionError - if the check fails and assertions are enabled both via the -ea JVM command line option and by setting AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS to false.

      • assertIfEnabled

        public static boolean assertIfEnabled(LongTriPredicate requirement,
                                      long value,
                                      long otherFirstValue,
                                      long otherSecondValue)
        Asserts that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherSecondValue satisfies the provided requirement if assertions is enabled (i.e. -ea) and AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS is false.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in private methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public private(long bar) {
     assertIfEnabled(between(), bar, 13, 42);
     this.bar = bar;
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        otherFirstValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        otherSecondValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        Returns:
        true
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null. There is no guarantee that this exception is thrown. For example, if assertions are not enabled, then the exception might not be thrown.
        AssertionError - if the check fails and assertions are enabled both via the -ea JVM command line option and by setting AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS to false.

      • failDescription

        public static String failDescription(LongPredicate requirement,
                                     long value)
        Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value did not satisfy the provided requirement.
        Parameters:
        requirement - to imposed on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        Returns:
        a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value did not satisfy the provided requirement
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.

      • failDescription

        public static String failDescription(LongBiPredicate requirement,
                                     long value,
                                     long otherValue)
        Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value and provided otherValue did not satisfy the provided requirement.
        Parameters:
        requirement - to imposed on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        otherValue - the other value to compare against the provided value
        Returns:
        a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value and provided otherValue did not satisfy the provided requirement
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.

      • failDescription

        public static String failDescription(LongTriPredicate requirement,
                                     long value,
                                     long otherFirstValue,
                                     long otherSecondValue)
        Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherFirstValue did not satisfy the provided requirement.
        Parameters:
        requirement - to imposed on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        Returns:
        a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value and provided otherValue did not satisfy the provided requirement
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.

      • positive

        public static LongPredicate positive()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is positive (i.e. value > 0).
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is positive (i.e. value > 0)

      • negative

        public static LongPredicate negative()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is negative (i.e. value < 0).
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is negative (i.e. value < 0)

      • nonNegative

        public static LongPredicate nonNegative()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is non-negative (i.e. value >= 0).

        This is equivalent to: negative().negate()

        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is non-negative (i.e. value >= 0)

      • zero

        public static LongPredicate zero()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is zero (i.e. value == 0).
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is zero (i.e. value == 0)

      • byteConvertible

        public static LongPredicate byteConvertible()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value can fit in a byte (i.e. value ∈ [-128, 127]").
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value can fit in a byte (i.e. value ∈ [-128, 127]")

      • shortConvertible

        public static LongPredicate shortConvertible()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value can fit in a short (i.e. value ∈ [-32768, 32767]").
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value can fit in a short (i.e. value ∈ [-32768, 32767]")

      • evenPowerOfTwo

        public static LongPredicate evenPowerOfTwo()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is an even power of two (i.e. log2(value) is an integer).
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is an even power of two (i.e. log2(value) is an integer)

      • equalTo

        public static LongBiPredicate equalTo()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is equal to another value.
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is equal to another value

      • greaterThan

        public static LongBiPredicate greaterThan()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is greater than to another value.
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is greater than to another value

      • greaterOrEqual

        public static LongBiPredicate greaterOrEqual()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is greater or equal to another value.
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is greater or equal to another value

      • lessThan

        public static LongBiPredicate lessThan()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is less than to another value.
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is less than to another value

      • lessOrEqual

        public static LongBiPredicate lessOrEqual()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is less or equal to another value.
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is less or equal to another value

      • betweenZeroAnd

        public static LongBiPredicate betweenZeroAnd()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another value (exclusive) (i.e value ∈ [0, other value) ).
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another value (exclusive) (i.e value ∈ [0, other value) )

      • betweenZeroAndClosed

        public static LongBiPredicate betweenZeroAndClosed()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another value (inclusive) (i.e value ∈ [0, other value] ).
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another value (inclusive) (i.e value ∈ [0, other value] )

      • powerOfTwo

        public static LongBiPredicate powerOfTwo()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is a power of two of another value (i.e. value = 2 ^ other value). For example, powerOfTwo().test(16, 4) is true because 2^4 is 16
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is an even power of two (i.e. an N exists such that value ^ N is an integer)

      • log2

        public static LongBiPredicate log2()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is log2 of another value (i.e. value = log2(other value) ).

        For example, log2().test(4, 16) is true because log2(16) is 4

        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is log2 of another value (i.e. value = log2(other value) ).

      • between

        public static LongTriPredicate between()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between another first value (inclusive) and another second value (exclusive) (i.e value ∈ [other first value , other second value) ).
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is between another first value (inclusive) and another second value (exclusive) (i.e value ∈ [other first value , other second value) )

      • betweenClosed

        public static LongTriPredicate betweenClosed()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between (closed) another first value (inclusive) and another second value (inclusive) (i.e value ∈ [other first value , other second value] ).
        Returns:
        a predicate that can test if a value is between (closed) another first value (inclusive) and another second value (inclusive) (i.e value ∈ [other first value , other second value] ).

      • betweenZeroAndReserving

        public static LongTriPredicate betweenZeroAndReserving()
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another first value (inclusive) whilst ensuring a value of size defined by another second value can fit. (i.e value ∈ [0, other first value - other second value] ).

        This predicate is useful when ensuring that a memory structure can be updated without exceeding its upper bounds. For example: 

         public static void putLong(byte[] bytes, long offset, long value) {
         assert nonNull(bytes);
         assert betweenZeroAndReserving.test(offset, bytes.length, Integer.BYTES);
         ...
        Returns:
        Returns a predicate that can test if a value is between zero and another first value (inclusive) whilst ensuring a value of size defined by another second value can fit. (i.e value ∈ [0, other first value - other second value] ).
